corpus.title: A Thriving City Hall Pillar — Richmond Civic Hackathon
corpus.description: Research corpus on Richmond's government operations and civic service delivery: resident service navigation via rva.gov and RVA311, and procurement contract review across City/State/Federal systems.

files.title: A Thriving City Hall — Decision Funnel
files.summary: Primary entry point for hackathon teams. Covers the three core questions, decision phases (0–9), guardrails, repo map, and Hackbot introduction. Read first for orientation.

files.title: Quick Start
files.summary: Minimal path from landing to MVP scope for teams with limited time before they need to make decisions.
recommended_audience: teams that need a clear starting point

files.title: Hackbot Agent Specification
files.summary: Full specification for Hackbot: personality, boot sequence, skill system, research corpus navigation instructions, guardrails, and decision logic. AI agents should read this before acting.

files.title: Research Corpus Guide
files.summary: Self-describing guide for humans and AI agents on how to navigate and use the research corpus. Covers canonical source of truth hierarchy, section structure, navigation decision tree, and agent behavior requirements.
recommended_audience: hackathon participants unfamiliar with the corpus

files.title: Maintenance Guide
files.summary: Instructions for adding, updating, and synchronizing research reports, metadata, indexes, and navigation files. Required reading before modifying the corpus structure.
recommended_audience: AI agents tasked with corpus updates

files.title: Hackbot Boot Prompt
files.summary: Initialization prompt for Hackbot. Contains the condensed boot sequence and initial framing for AI agent sessions.

files.title: Claude Role Definition
files.summary: Role and operating rules for Claude AI in this repository: civic research guide, Socratic coach, MVP advisor for the City Hall pillar. Defines what Claude should and should not do, preferred solution patterns, and the default task sequence.

files.title: Evidence Log — A Thriving City Hall
files.summary: Tracking table for 15 factual claims about Richmond City Hall systems: RVA311, rva.gov, City Contracts, and related data sources. Most entries are currently unverified and need official source confirmation.

files.title: Research Hub and Executive Brief
files.summary: Primary synthesized research hub: executive brief and promoted findings from the research corpus. Best single document for teams needing a comprehensive summary quickly.
recommended_audience: anyone needing the executive summary of all research

files.title: Pillar Overview
files.summary: Overview of the A Thriving City Hall pillar problem space: government service delivery, resident access to City services, staff efficiency, and procurement integrity. Foundational context for all research.

files.title: MAP Alignment
files.summary: Alignment of this pillar with the Mayor's Action Plan (MAP). Explains the civic and strategic context for hackathon solutions targeting City Hall operations.

files.title: Blue-Sky Problem Statements
files.summary: Broad, unconstrained problem statements for exploratory ideation in the City Hall pillar. Use for teams in orientation wanting a wide problem canvas before narrowing.

files.title: Targeted Problem Statements (Scored)
files.summary: Two scored, rubric-evaluated problem statements: Resident Service Navigation (27/32 Strong) and Procurement Risk Review (22/32 Needs Work). Includes rubric dimensions, champion status, and quick-kill flags.

files.title: Data Index
files.summary: Index of available data sources for the A Thriving City Hall pillar. Points to the source inventory CSV and explains data categories.

files.title: Source Inventory (CSV)
files.summary: Structured inventory of public data sources with access mode, key fields, and notes. Includes City Contracts Socrata (xqn7-jvv2), RVA311 historical (vgg4-hjn8), eVA, SAM.gov, and rva.gov.

files.title: User Journeys
files.summary: Structured journey maps for primary City Hall user personas: resident seeking a service, 311 call center staff, and procurement staff reviewing contracts. Useful for MVP design and demo narrative.

files.title: Prototype Recommendations
files.summary: Seeded MVP concepts for the City Hall pillar with rationale: Contract Expiry Dashboard, Plain-Language 311 Helper, and Service Pathway Mapper. Includes recommended starting direction.

files.title: Benchmark Scan
files.summary: Scan of comparable civic tech tools from other jurisdictions. Synthesizes E section findings on prior art for government service navigation and procurement transparency tools.

files.title: City Hall Tech Inventory
files.summary: Inventory of the City of Richmond's current technology stack relevant to the hackathon: RVA311 (AvePoint/Dynamics 365), rva.gov (Drupal/Acquia), City Contracts (Socrata), eVA, SAM.gov, and related systems.

files.title: Continuation Plan
files.summary: Post-hackathon pathways for continuing City Hall work: potential partners, city departments, and next steps for each prototype direction.

files.title: MVP Shapes
files.summary: Catalog of MVP build patterns appropriate for the City Hall pillar: service navigator, contract expiry dashboard, pathway mapper. Includes scope, tech stack, and demo path for each shape.

files.title: Recommended Architectures
files.summary: Technical architecture recommendations for City Hall builds: static decision tree, Socrata-backed dashboard, rva.gov content index. Covers frontend, backend, and data layer choices.

files.title: Demo Advice
files.summary: Practical advice for demo preparation and delivery in the City Hall pillar: timing, framing, judge expectations, and avoiding overclaims about civic tech capabilities.

files.title: Skill: repo_memory
files.summary: Skill for reconstructing repository state: reads project artifacts, detects team stage, and summarizes progress for Hackbot context reconstruction.

files.title: Skill: problem_scoping
files.summary: Skill for helping teams choose a strong problem: analyzes problem statements, ranks opportunities, and identifies realistic build directions for City Hall.

files.title: Skill: research_runner
files.summary: Skill for executing research prompts and collecting findings: runs prompts, extracts sources, summarizes evidence.

files.title: Skill: dataset_mapper
files.summary: Skill for identifying usable datasets: scans the data directory, identifies schemas, and highlights gaps for City Hall data sources.

files.title: Skill: opportunity_mapper
files.summary: Skill for translating research into buildable solution opportunities: matches City Hall problems to solution patterns and suggests MVP directions.

files.title: Skill: mvp_designer
files.summary: Skill for turning an opportunity into a concrete MVP: produces user flow, architecture, and scope boundaries for City Hall prototypes.

files.title: Skill: risk_review
files.summary: Skill for preventing civic-tech mistakes: checks for hallucinated services, legal claims, incorrect routing logic, and misleading language in City Hall tools.

files.title: Skill: demo_coach
files.summary: Skill for helping teams present effectively: produces demo script, narrative structure, and judge-facing explanation for City Hall solutions.

files.title: Skill: repo_librarian
files.summary: Skill for helping users navigate repository knowledge: answers questions about where City Hall datasets, problem statements, and templates live.

files.title: Skill: continuity_planner
files.summary: Skill for identifying post-hackathon continuation opportunities: finds potential City partners, artifacts worth continuing, and next steps for City Hall projects.
recommended_audience: teams planning beyond the hackathon weekend

files.title: Skill: research_corpus_navigation
files.summary: Skill for mapping user questions to the correct corpus section and specific research reports before attempting to answer. Uses index.json key_terms matching and section classification.

files.title: Skill: cross_report_synthesis
files.summary: Skill for synthesizing findings across multiple research reports with source traceability, tension identification, and explicit gap documentation.

files.title: Skill: evidence_grounded_answering
files.summary: Skill for answering questions using only content present in this repository corpus, with explicit claim classification (confirmed/stated/uncertain/not found) and anti-fabrication checks.
recommended_audience: AI agents making factual claims about Richmond City Hall

files.title: Skill: report_update_protocol
files.summary: Skill for safely adding or updating research reports and keeping indexes, manifests, and navigation files synchronized. Includes 7-step process with validation checklist.

files.title: Working Direction Note Template
files.summary: Team template for capturing a 5-bullet working direction early in orientation: pillar, candidate problem, likely user, why it matters, why it's weekend-buildable, biggest uncertainty.

files.title: Decision Memo Template
files.summary: Template for capturing the problem selection decision: chosen problem, user, rationale, why not the others, and risks.

files.title: Project One-Pager Template
files.summary: One-page project scope document template: must-haves, mockables, data, AI role, limits, and demo path. Used for scope lock in Phase 5.

files.title: Team Profile Template
files.summary: Template for the team profile file required by Hackbot before it can operate. Covers team name, members, roles, skills, availability, communication preferences, and constraints.

files.title: Research Corpus Index
files.summary: Machine-readable JSON index of all 51 research reports in the corpus. Each entry includes id, section, title, summary, and key_terms for navigation and retrieval. See individual research/*.md files for authoritative content.
